# Playwright CLI skill (installed, not hand-edited)

This directory holds the Playwright CLI skill that teaches coding agents how to
drive a real browser from the terminal and how to run/debug Playwright tests.

Do NOT hand-write the skill content. Install it from the official source so it
stays current with the CLI you have installed.

## Install the CLI, then the skills

```bash
# 1. Install the Playwright CLI globally (binary is `playwright-cli`).
npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest

# 2. Install the skills for whatever coding agent you use. This writes the
#    current SKILL.md (and its reference guides) into the agent's skills folder.
playwright-cli install --skills
```

`playwright-cli install --skills` is the supported way to (re)install the skill.
It always pulls the version that matches your installed CLI, so the documented
flags never drift from the binary. Re-run it after upgrading the CLI.

For Claude Code you can alternatively install via the plugin marketplace:

```bash
/plugin marketplace add microsoft/playwright-cli
/plugin install playwright-cli
```

## What the skill gives the agent

The skill ships reference guides for: running and debugging tests, request
mocking, browser session management, storage state (cookies/localStorage), and
spec-driven test generation (`plan` -> `generate` -> `heal`). Once installed, the
agent reads the `SKILL.md` once and uses the CLI vocabulary correctly instead of
guessing flags.

You can also run skills-less: point the agent at the CLI and it reads
`playwright-cli --help` on its own. The skill just makes it faster and steadier,
and uses fewer tokens because the agent stops trial-and-erroring flags.

> Note: the Playwright **CLI** (`playwright-cli`, browser driving + `plan`/
> `generate`/`heal`) is distinct from Playwright **Test Agents** (planner/
> generator/healer definitions generated by `npx playwright init-agents`, see
> `agents/README.md`). They are complementary: the Test Agents orchestrate the
> authoring loop; the CLI is the low-level, token-efficient browser tool they and
> you drive.
